step_1_context.text: We typically use a passive rather than an active in the following situations.

step_2_explanation.simple_rule: We typically use a passive rather than an active in the following situations.
step_2_explanation.formula: Note also that some verbs have related nouns which express the same meaning.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake: In English we usually prefer to put the topic (what is already being talked about) at the beginning of a sentence (or clause) and a comment on that topic at the end.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 22.1.1 They built the main stadium in under three years. (Start: The main stadium...)
options: The main stadium was built in under three years.
The main stadium were built in under three years.
The main stadium had been built in under three years.
The main stadium builds in under three years.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: The main stadium was built in under three years.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Past simple passive: was/were + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 22.1.2 They have designed the main stadium to accommodate many different sports.
options: The main stadium has been designed to accommodate many different sports.
The main stadium have been designed to accommodate many different sports.
The main stadium were designed to accommodate many different sports.
The main stadium has been design to accommodate many different sports.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: The main stadium has been designed to accommodate many different sports.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Present perfect passive: has/have been + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 22.1.3 They will take down the temporary stands after the Games.
options: The temporary stands will be taken down after the Games.
The temporary stands will taken down after the Games.
The temporary stands would be taken down after the Games.
The temporary stands being taken down after the Games.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: The temporary stands will be taken down after the Games.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Future simple passive: will be + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 22.1.4 They will have completed the basketball arena by the end of May.
options: The basketball arena will have been completed by the end of May.
The basketball arena will be completed by the end of May.
The basketball arena will have completed by the end of May.
The basketball arena would have been completed by the end of May.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: The basketball arena will have been completed by the end of May.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Future perfect passive: will have been + past participle.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 22.1.5 They are holding the rowing competition on the River Nene.
options: The rowing competition is being held on the River Nene.
The rowing competition are being held on the River Nene.
The rowing competition is being hold on the River Nene.
The rowing competition was being held on the River Nene.
step_3_interactive_exercises.correct_answer: The rowing competition is being held on the River Nene.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Present continuous passive: is/are being + past participle.
